items.title: June 14, 1990: Vinnie Johnson hits the game-winner with 0.7 seconds to go in Game 5, and the Pistons win their second-straight NBA Title with a 92-90 victory in Portland.
items.author: NBA Cobwebs
items.body_excerpt:
June 14, 1990: Vinnie Johnson hits the game-winner with 0.7 seconds to go in Game 5, and the Pistons win their second-straight NBA Title with a 92-90 victory in Portland.

Johnson had 16 PTS off the bench, and Finals MVP Isiah Thomas scored 29 PTS (13-20 FG/3-3 3P) for Detroit.

items.title: Hollander POTD Dan Godfread. Evansville C was undrafted in 90. Signed w/MIN in December 1990 & stuck for the sseason but only played 20 minutes total (1.3 ppg 0.2 rpg).  Signed w/HOU but lasted a week before being waived ending NBA career. Played in minors & abroad from 91-00.

Those conversations flatten reality.

Sometimes it's OK to just appreciate what happened.

Jordan Clarkson is eighth at +5.2. Miles McBride is ninth at +3.0. Mitchell Robinson is tenth at -12.2.

items.title: Highest plus/minus in a single postseason in NBA history:
items.author: Tommy Beer
items.body_excerpt:
Highest plus/minus in a single postseason in NBA history:

1. Karl-Anthony Towns: +258 (2026)
2. Steph Curry: +244 (2017)
3. Jalen Brunson: +234 (2026)
4. Draymond Green: +227 (2017)
5. Kobe Bryant: +213 (2001)
6. LeBron James: +210 (2016)
7. Kevin Durant: +206 (2018)

items.title: The De'Aaron Fox discourse is unfortunate.
items.author: Law Murray 📘
items.body_excerpt:
The De'Aaron Fox discourse is unfortunate. 

First two rounds, Fox averaged 18.8 points on 46.2% FGs, shot 34.6% 3s. Spurs went 8-3 in those games.

Fox misses first 2 games of WCF with ankle injury that we all saw, then comes back and shot 35.3% FGs, 24% 3s.

items.title: The average draft position of Last 8 Finals MVPs?
items.author: NBA Stat
items.body_excerpt:
The average draft position of Last 8 Finals MVPs? 

Picked 15th.

2026: Brunson (picked 33)
2025: SGA (11)
2024: J Brown (3)
2023: Jokic (41)
2022: Curry (7)
2021: Giannis (15)
2020: LeBron (1)
2019: Kawhi (15)

(via Steve Ilardi)
#NBA
